Package Index | Class Index
Summary: Ctors | Methods | Inherited Methods | [Expand All]
public class

Preconditions

extends Object
java.lang.Object
   ↳ com.android.internal.util.Preconditions

Class Overview

Simple static methods to be called at the start of your own methods to verify correct arguments and state.

Summary

Public Constructors
Preconditions()
Public Methods
static void checkArgument(boolean expression, Object errorMessage)
Ensures that an expression checking an argument is true.
static void checkArgument(boolean expression)
Ensures that an expression checking an argument is true.
static void checkArgument(boolean expression, String messageTemplate, Object... messageArgs)
Ensures that an expression checking an argument is true.
static float checkArgumentFinite(float value, String valueName)
Ensures that the argument floating point value is a finite number.
static double checkArgumentInRange(double value, double lower, double upper, String valueName)
Ensures that the argument floating point value is within the inclusive range.
static float checkArgumentInRange(float value, float lower, float upper, String valueName)
Ensures that the argument floating point value is within the inclusive range.
static long checkArgumentInRange(long value, long lower, long upper, String valueName)
Ensures that the argument long value is within the inclusive range.
static int checkArgumentInRange(int value, int lower, int upper, String valueName)
Ensures that the argument int value is within the inclusive range.
static String checkArgumentIsSupported(String[] supportedValues, String value)
Ensures that argument value is one of supportedValues.
static float checkArgumentNonNegative(float value, String errorMessage)
Ensures that the argument floating point value is non-negative (greater than or equal to 0).
static int checkArgumentNonnegative(int value)
Ensures that that the argument numeric value is non-negative (greater than or equal to 0).
static long checkArgumentNonnegative(long value)
Ensures that that the argument numeric value is non-negative (greater than or equal to 0).
static int checkArgumentNonnegative(int value, String errorMessage)
Ensures that that the argument numeric value is non-negative (greater than or equal to 0).
static long checkArgumentNonnegative(long value, String errorMessage)
Ensures that that the argument numeric value is non-negative (greater than or equal to 0).
static float checkArgumentPositive(float value, String errorMessage)
Ensures that the argument floating point value is positive (greater than 0).
static int checkArgumentPositive(int value, String errorMessage)
Ensures that that the argument numeric value is positive (greater than 0).
static int[] checkArrayElementsInRange(int[] value, int lower, int upper, String valueName)
Ensures that all elements in the argument integer array are within the inclusive range
static float[] checkArrayElementsInRange(float[] value, float lower, float upper, String valueName)
Ensures that all elements in the argument floating point array are within the inclusive range

While this can be used to range check against +/- infinity, note that all NaN numbers will always be out of range.

static <T> T[] checkArrayElementsNotNull(T[] value, String valueName)
Ensures that the array is not null, and none of its elements are null.
static byte[] checkByteArrayNotEmpty(byte[] value, String valueName)
Ensures that the given byte array is not null, and contains at least one element.
static void checkCallAuthorization(boolean expression, String messageTemplate, Object... messageArgs)
Ensures the truth of an expression involving whether the calling identity is authorized to call the calling method.
static void checkCallAuthorization(boolean expression)
Ensures the truth of an expression involving whether the calling identity is authorized to call the calling method.
static void checkCallAuthorization(boolean expression, String message)
Ensures the truth of an expression involving whether the calling identity is authorized to call the calling method.
static void checkCallingUser(boolean expression)
Ensures the truth of an expression involving whether the calling user is authorized to call the calling method.
static <C extends Collection<T>, T> C checkCollectionElementsNotNull(C value, String valueName)
Ensures that the Collection is not null, and none of its elements are null.
static <T> Collection<T> checkCollectionNotEmpty(Collection<T> value, String valueName)
Ensures that the Collection is not null, and contains at least one element.
static int checkFlagsArgument(int requestedFlags, int allowedFlags)
Check the requested flags, throwing if any requested flags are outside the allowed set.
static <T> T checkNotNull(T reference, String messageTemplate, Object... messageArgs)
Ensures that an object reference passed as a parameter to the calling method is not null.
static <T> T checkNotNull(T reference)
This method is deprecated. - use ERROR(/java.util.Objects.requireNonNull) instead.
static <T> T checkNotNull(T reference, Object errorMessage)
This method is deprecated. - use Objects.requireNonNull(T) instead.
static void checkState(boolean expression, String errorMessage)
Ensures the truth of an expression involving the state of the calling instance, but not involving any parameters to the calling method.
static void checkState(boolean expression)
Ensures the truth of an expression involving the state of the calling instance, but not involving any parameters to the calling method.
static void checkState(boolean expression, String messageTemplate, Object... messageArgs)
Ensures the truth of an expression involving the state of the calling instance, but not involving any parameters to the calling method.
static <T extends CharSequence> T checkStringNotEmpty(T string, Object errorMessage)
Ensures that an string reference passed as a parameter to the calling method is not empty.
static <T extends CharSequence> T checkStringNotEmpty(T string, String messageTemplate, Object... messageArgs)
Ensures that an string reference passed as a parameter to the calling method is not empty.
static <T extends CharSequence> T checkStringNotEmpty(T string)
Ensures that an string reference passed as a parameter to the calling method is not empty.
static <T> T requireNonNullViaRavenwoodRule(T t)
Throws an exception that guides developers to configure a RavenwoodRule when the given argument is null.

Public Constructors

public Preconditions ()

Public Methods

public static void checkArgument (boolean expression, Object errorMessage)

Ensures that an expression checking an argument is true.

Parameters
expression boolean: the expression to check
errorMessage Object: the exception message to use if the check fails; will be converted to a string using String.valueOf(Object)
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if expression is false

public static void checkArgument (boolean expression)

Ensures that an expression checking an argument is true.

Parameters
expression boolean: the expression to check
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if expression is false

public static void checkArgument (boolean expression, String messageTemplate, Object... messageArgs)

Ensures that an expression checking an argument is true.

Parameters
expression boolean: the expression to check
messageTemplate String: a printf-style message template to use if the check fails; will be converted to a string using String.format(String, Object)
messageArgs Object: arguments for messageTemplate
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if expression is false

public static float checkArgumentFinite (float value, String valueName)

Ensures that the argument floating point value is a finite number.

A finite number is defined to be both representable (that is, not NaN) and not infinite (that is neither positive or negative infinity).

Parameters
value float: a floating point value
valueName String: the name of the argument to use if the check fails
Returns
float the validated floating point value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if value was not finite

public static double checkArgumentInRange (double value, double lower, double upper, String valueName)

Ensures that the argument floating point value is within the inclusive range.

While this can be used to range check against +/- infinity, note that all NaN numbers will always be out of range.

Parameters
value double: a floating point value
lower double: the lower endpoint of the inclusive range
upper double: the upper endpoint of the inclusive range
valueName String: the name of the argument to use if the check fails
Returns
double the validated floating point value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if value was not within the range

public static float checkArgumentInRange (float value, float lower, float upper, String valueName)

Ensures that the argument floating point value is within the inclusive range.

While this can be used to range check against +/- infinity, note that all NaN numbers will always be out of range.

Parameters
value float: a floating point value
lower float: the lower endpoint of the inclusive range
upper float: the upper endpoint of the inclusive range
valueName String: the name of the argument to use if the check fails
Returns
float the validated floating point value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if value was not within the range

public static long checkArgumentInRange (long value, long lower, long upper, String valueName)

Ensures that the argument long value is within the inclusive range.

Parameters
value long: a long value
lower long: the lower endpoint of the inclusive range
upper long: the upper endpoint of the inclusive range
valueName String: the name of the argument to use if the check fails
Returns
long the validated long value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if value was not within the range

public static int checkArgumentInRange (int value, int lower, int upper, String valueName)

Ensures that the argument int value is within the inclusive range.

Parameters
value int: a int value
lower int: the lower endpoint of the inclusive range
upper int: the upper endpoint of the inclusive range
valueName String: the name of the argument to use if the check fails
Returns
int the validated int value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if value was not within the range

public static String checkArgumentIsSupported (String[] supportedValues, String value)

Ensures that argument value is one of supportedValues.

Parameters
supportedValues String: an array of string values
value String: a string value
Returns
String the validated value
Throws
NullPointerException if either value or supportedValues is null
IllegalArgumentException if the value is not in supportedValues

public static float checkArgumentNonNegative (float value, String errorMessage)

Ensures that the argument floating point value is non-negative (greater than or equal to 0).

Parameters
value float: a floating point value
errorMessage String: the exteption message to use if the check fails
Returns
float the validated numeric value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if value was negative

public static int checkArgumentNonnegative (int value)

Ensures that that the argument numeric value is non-negative (greater than or equal to 0).

Parameters
value int: a numeric int value
Returns
int the validated numeric value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if value was negative

public static long checkArgumentNonnegative (long value)

Ensures that that the argument numeric value is non-negative (greater than or equal to 0).

Parameters
value long: a numeric long value
Returns
long the validated numeric value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if value was negative

public static int checkArgumentNonnegative (int value, String errorMessage)

Ensures that that the argument numeric value is non-negative (greater than or equal to 0).

Parameters
value int: a numeric int value
errorMessage String: the exception message to use if the check fails
Returns
int the validated numeric value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if value was negative

public static long checkArgumentNonnegative (long value, String errorMessage)

Ensures that that the argument numeric value is non-negative (greater than or equal to 0).

Parameters
value long: a numeric long value
errorMessage String: the exception message to use if the check fails
Returns
long the validated numeric value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if value was negative

public static float checkArgumentPositive (float value, String errorMessage)

Ensures that the argument floating point value is positive (greater than 0).

Parameters
value float: a floating point value
errorMessage String: the exteption message to use if the check fails
Returns
float the validated numeric value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if value was not positive

public static int checkArgumentPositive (int value, String errorMessage)

Ensures that that the argument numeric value is positive (greater than 0).

Parameters
value int: a numeric int value
errorMessage String: the exception message to use if the check fails
Returns
int the validated numeric value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if value was not positive

public static int[] checkArrayElementsInRange (int[] value, int lower, int upper, String valueName)

Ensures that all elements in the argument integer array are within the inclusive range

Parameters
value int: an integer array of values
lower int: the lower endpoint of the inclusive range
upper int: the upper endpoint of the inclusive range
valueName String: the name of the argument to use if the check fails
Returns
int[] the validated integer array
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if any of the elements in value were out of range
NullPointerException if the value was null

public static float[] checkArrayElementsInRange (float[] value, float lower, float upper, String valueName)

Ensures that all elements in the argument floating point array are within the inclusive range

While this can be used to range check against +/- infinity, note that all NaN numbers will always be out of range.

Parameters
value float: a floating point array of values
lower float: the lower endpoint of the inclusive range
upper float: the upper endpoint of the inclusive range
valueName String: the name of the argument to use if the check fails
Returns
float[] the validated floating point value
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if any of the elements in value were out of range
NullPointerException if the value was null

public static T[] checkArrayElementsNotNull (T[] value, String valueName)

Ensures that the array is not null, and none of its elements are null.

Parameters
value T: an array of boxed objects
valueName String: the name of the argument to use if the check fails
Returns
T[] the validated array
Throws
NullPointerException if the value or any of its elements were null

public static byte[] checkByteArrayNotEmpty (byte[] value, String valueName)

Ensures that the given byte array is not null, and contains at least one element.

Parameters
value byte: an array of elements.
valueName String: the name of the argument to use if the check fails.
Returns
byte[] the validated array
Throws
NullPointerException if the value was null
IllegalArgumentException if the value was empty

public static void checkCallAuthorization (boolean expression, String messageTemplate, Object... messageArgs)

Ensures the truth of an expression involving whether the calling identity is authorized to call the calling method.

Parameters
expression boolean: a boolean expression
messageTemplate String: a printf-style message template to use if the check fails; will be converted to a string using String.format(String, Object)
messageArgs Object: arguments for messageTemplate
Throws
SecurityException if expression is false

public static void checkCallAuthorization (boolean expression)

Ensures the truth of an expression involving whether the calling identity is authorized to call the calling method.

Parameters
expression boolean: a boolean expression
Throws
SecurityException if expression is false

public static void checkCallAuthorization (boolean expression, String message)

Ensures the truth of an expression involving whether the calling identity is authorized to call the calling method.

Parameters
expression boolean: a boolean expression
message String: the message of the security exception to be thrown
Throws
SecurityException if expression is false

public static void checkCallingUser (boolean expression)

Ensures the truth of an expression involving whether the calling user is authorized to call the calling method.

Parameters
expression boolean: a boolean expression
Throws
SecurityException if expression is false

public static C checkCollectionElementsNotNull (C value, String valueName)

Ensures that the Collection is not null, and none of its elements are null.

Parameters
value C: a Collection of boxed objects
valueName String: the name of the argument to use if the check fails
Returns
C the validated Collection
Throws
NullPointerException if the value or any of its elements were null

public static Collection<T> checkCollectionNotEmpty (Collection<T> value, String valueName)

Ensures that the Collection is not null, and contains at least one element.

Parameters
value Collection: a Collection of boxed elements.
valueName String: the name of the argument to use if the check fails.
Returns
Collection<T> the validated Collection
Throws
NullPointerException if the value was null
IllegalArgumentException if the value was empty

public static int checkFlagsArgument (int requestedFlags, int allowedFlags)

Check the requested flags, throwing if any requested flags are outside the allowed set.

Parameters
requestedFlags int
allowedFlags int
Returns
int the validated requested flags.

public static T checkNotNull (T reference, String messageTemplate, Object... messageArgs)

Ensures that an object reference passed as a parameter to the calling method is not null.

Parameters
reference T
messageTemplate String: a printf-style message template to use if the check fails; will be converted to a string using String.format(String, Object)
messageArgs Object: arguments for messageTemplate
Returns
T
Throws
NullPointerException if reference is null

public static T checkNotNull (T reference)

This method is deprecated.
- use ERROR(/java.util.Objects.requireNonNull) instead.

Ensures that an object reference passed as a parameter to the calling method is not null.

Parameters
reference T: an object reference
Returns
T the non-null reference that was validated
Throws
NullPointerException if reference is null

public static T checkNotNull (T reference, Object errorMessage)

This method is deprecated.
- use Objects.requireNonNull(T) instead.

Ensures that an object reference passed as a parameter to the calling method is not null.

Parameters
reference T: an object reference
errorMessage Object: the exception message to use if the check fails; will be converted to a string using String.valueOf(Object)
Returns
T the non-null reference that was validated
Throws
NullPointerException if reference is null

public static void checkState (boolean expression, String errorMessage)

Ensures the truth of an expression involving the state of the calling instance, but not involving any parameters to the calling method.

Parameters
expression boolean: a boolean expression
errorMessage String: the exception message to use if the check fails; will be converted to a string using String.valueOf(Object)
Throws
IllegalStateException if expression is false

public static void checkState (boolean expression)

Ensures the truth of an expression involving the state of the calling instance, but not involving any parameters to the calling method.

Parameters
expression boolean: a boolean expression
Throws
IllegalStateException if expression is false

public static void checkState (boolean expression, String messageTemplate, Object... messageArgs)

Ensures the truth of an expression involving the state of the calling instance, but not involving any parameters to the calling method.

Parameters
expression boolean: a boolean expression
messageTemplate String: a printf-style message template to use if the check fails; will be converted to a string using String.format(String, Object)
messageArgs Object: arguments for messageTemplate
Throws
IllegalStateException if expression is false

public static T checkStringNotEmpty (T string, Object errorMessage)

Ensures that an string reference passed as a parameter to the calling method is not empty.

Parameters
string T: an string reference
errorMessage Object: the exception message to use if the check fails; will be converted to a string using String.valueOf(Object)
Returns
T the string reference that was validated
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if string is empty

public static T checkStringNotEmpty (T string, String messageTemplate, Object... messageArgs)

Ensures that an string reference passed as a parameter to the calling method is not empty.

Parameters
string T: an string reference
messageTemplate String: a printf-style message template to use if the check fails; will be converted to a string using String.format(String, Object)
messageArgs Object: arguments for messageTemplate
Returns
T the string reference that was validated
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if string is empty

public static T checkStringNotEmpty (T string)

Ensures that an string reference passed as a parameter to the calling method is not empty.

Parameters
string T: an string reference
Returns
T the string reference that was validated
Throws
IllegalArgumentException if string is empty

public static T requireNonNullViaRavenwoodRule (T t)

Throws an exception that guides developers to configure a RavenwoodRule when the given argument is null.

Parameters
t T
Returns
T
